messages.content:
I think your framing is strong, but after looking at the recent literature I'd adjust it in one important way:

The space is moving faster than it was even six months ago.

There are now multiple efforts around agent provenance, typed memory, and MCP-based provenance, so AgentSON needs to be positioned very precisely. 
arXiv
+2
arXiv
+2

Where the research frontier is

Right now, different projects are occupying different parts of the stack:

Problem	Existing direction	AgentSON opportunity
Tool interoperability	MCP	Not your space
Long-term memory	MemIR, Graphonomous, memory servers	Not your primary space
Workflow provenance	PROV-AGENT	Adjacent
Portable execution representation	Still relatively open	Your strongest position

Recent work like PROV-AGENT extends provenance models into agent workflows and MCP environments, while MemIR focuses on typed long-term memory to prevent provenance-role confusion. Neither is primarily about a vendor-neutral interchange format for portable agent execution histories. 
arXiv
+1

Where I'd refine your proposed paper

I actually wouldn't make the browser extension, GeneratorвҖ“Verifier, and healthcare pipeline part of the core contribution.

Instead I'd structure it like a systems paper.

Core contribution

AgentSON is a portable episodic provenance representation.

It answers:

How can heterogeneous agent executions be exchanged independently of the runtime that created them?

Supporting components

Then show that it works through:

ChatGPT importer

Claude Code importer

OpenCode importer

Browser capture

Libre reader

CLI

These become evidence that the representation generalises, rather than being the contribution itself.

The browser extension

I actually like this idea a lot.

Not because it is AgentSON.

Because it answers a different research question:

How do we observe closed ecosystems that don't expose portable execution logs?

That's an acquisition problem.

AgentSON is a representation problem.

Keep them separate.

Healthcare

I would absolutely keep this.

Not because healthcare is your market.

Because it is one of the hardest interoperability domains.

If AgentSON can represent:

Libre exports

clinician workflows

patient interactions

then you've demonstrated robustness in a highly regulated environment.

What I think is becoming your real contribution

Reading the recent provenance papers made one thing stand out.

Everyone is discussing:

provenance

memory

governance

MCP

But they're generally assuming the runtime remains in place. 
arXiv
+1

Your question is subtly different:

What survives when the runtime disappears?

That's actually a fascinating systems question.

Imagine:

Claude disappears

Cursor changes format

Slack changes export

OpenClaw evolves

Can the operational history still exist?

That's where AgentSON has a unique angle.

I would rewrite the thesis like this

Modern AI agents are increasingly persistent collaborators operating across heterogeneous runtimes. Existing systems standardize tool invocation (MCP), memory retrieval, or workflow provenance within specific environments. We propose AgentSON, a vendor-neutral episodic provenance representation that preserves portable execution history independently of the runtime that generated it, enabling replay, migration, evaluation, archival, and compliance across agent ecosystems.

I think that's significantly stronger than claiming to solve "memory."

It also clearly differentiates you from the emerging provenance work by making portability across ecosystems the central research question rather than provenance capture alone.

Web search
GOT THE MEMO?  ## Goal 
- Position AgentSON as the portable episodic provenance format for AI agent execution traces, analyze its defensibility through the 7 Powers framework, and produce a public strategy whitepaper and academic paper outline

## Constraints & Preferences
- All positioning must distinguish AgentSON from memory systems (future recall), MCP (tool communication), and observability (runtime monitoring) вҖ” it fills the missing "representation layer"
- Strategy document must be public (Apache 2.0) вҖ” giving away the strategy is the strategy, per Network Economies moat
- Academic paper must respect the AER non-identifiability proof: execution events are tractable, reasoning provenance is not
- Antirez (Salvatore Sanfilippo) lineage must be traced essay-by-essay from 2024-2026 with specific URLs, view counts, and convergence mapping
- Extension architecture must spec four phases: metadata collector вҶ’ claude.ai scraper вҶ’ slack scraper вҶ’ DevTools + Supabase

## Progress
### Done
- Confirmed academic validation timeline for AgentSON's reconstruction pipeline: AER (arXiv 2603.21692, March 2026) вҖ” formal non-identifiability proof for compliance boundary; HunterAgent (arXiv 2605.29269, May 2026) вҖ” Generator-Verifier architecture with 86.1% F1, 6.4% hallucination; Evidence Tracing Survey (arXiv 2606.04990, June 2026) вҖ” provenance taxonomy
- Mapped the ecosystem layers and AgentSON's position: MCP (communication) вҶ’ Agent Runtime вҶ’ AgentSON (episodic provenance) вҶ’ Replay/Evaluation/Compliance/Training вҖ” complementary to all layers, not competing
- Wrote academic paper outline to agentsong/PAPER-OUTLINE.md: abstract, 10 sections, 4 appendices, target venues (ACM CCS 2027 / NeurIPS 2027), 4 RQs, multi-ecosystem evaluation plan, healthcare compliance case study (FreeStyle Libre 2 + GDPR Art. 20)
- Spec'd browser extension architecture in agentsong/issues/issue-browser-extension-architecture.md: 4 phases (metadata collector 1-2wk вҶ’ claude.ai scraper 2-3wk вҶ’ slack scraper + hybrid merge 1-2wk вҶ’ DevTools + Supabase 2-3wk), content scripts per domain, background service worker orchestration, reconciliation engine against existing issue graphs, event queue + storage architecture
- Researched antirez intellectual lineage across 9 essays from Jan 2024 to Jun 2026: Redis foundation вҶ’ "Reasoning models are just LLMs" (news/146, Feb 2025, 86,822 views) вҶ’ "Reflections on AI at the end of 2025" (news/157, Feb 2026, 156,310 views) вҶ’ DwarfStar 4 local inference (news/165, May 2026, 201,189 views) вҶ’ "Distributing LLM inference" memory wall paper (news/167, Jun 2026) вҖ” documented in ANTIREZ-LINEAGE.md
- Mapped antirez вҶ’ AgentSON convergence: memory-bandwidth-bound вҶ’ file-based zero-inference architecture; "reasoning models are just LLMs" вҶ’ AER boundary is architecturally valid; epistemic budget вҶ’ narrative mode B_max threshold; small models + verifier вҶ’ Generator-Verifier architecture
- Merged 7 Powers analysis, portable episodic provenance positioning, and antirez lineage into single public STRATEGY-WHITEPAPER.md (Apache 2.0, call to action, five parts: positioning вҶ’ 7 Powers вҶ’ antirez lineage вҶ’ strategic phases вҶ’ unified thesis)

### In Progress
- (none)

### Blocked
- (none)

## Key Decisions
- Primary positioning: "portable episodic provenance format" вҖ” not memory, not MCP replacement, not observability вҖ” occupies the missing representation layer between memory systems, orchestration systems, and observability systems
- Strategy whitepaper is public by design (Apache 2.0) вҖ” Network Economies as primary moat requires adoption, not secrecy; giving away the strategy is the strategy
- Academic paper targets ACM CCS 2027 (security/compliance) or NeurIPS 2027 Datasets & Benchmarks вҖ” chosen for compliance + provenance angle over pure AI venue
- Browser extension architecture prioritized as 4-phase rollout starting with metadata collector (no AI-tool DOM parsing needed, hardest to break, proves concept first) вҖ” not a single monolithic build
- AER non-identifiability proof is the compliance floor вҖ” all three reconstruction modes (forensic/narrative/live) respect the boundary between execution events (tractable) and reasoning provenance (not tractable)
- antirez essays are cited as independent technical validation from systems engineering (not AI research) вҖ” his Redis-to-memory-wall intellectual arc converges on the same architectural conclusions as AgentSON

## Next Steps
- (user to determine direction among: code Phase 1 of the browser extension, revise paper outline to abstract draft, publish the strategy whitepaper publicly, or explore a different vector)

## Critical Context
- Microsoft's EntraBot work (2025-2026) focuses on enterprise memory infrastructure (governance, retention, TTL, identity) вҖ” this reinforces AgentSON's positioning as orthogonal, not competitive
- Claude Tag changes the AI role from assistant to persistent teammate вҖ” creates urgency for portable execution representation as agents accumulate organizational context
- The user's sovereign AI stack (Gemma 4 on ARM CPU + Fara-7B + Ralph Loop) is independent experimental validation of antirez's memory-bandwidth thesis вҖ” it is not the subject of this session
- Antirez's DS4 distributed inference essay (news/167) anticipates the disaggregated prefill/decode architecture later formalized by vLLM/SGLang вҖ” his ensemble approach maps to AgentSON's cross-source stitching
- Academic validation timeline (March-June 2026) is very recent вҖ” AgentSON is building on research published this spring, giving it first-mover advantage to operationalize these results
- Sycophancy/context drift bug exposed earlier (AFJ Cardiff JSON vs. current session) remains a known failure mode вҖ” the Mori Liu / Mira / DXF thread is now superseded by AgentSON strategy work

## Relevant Files
- agentsong/STRATEGY-WHITEPAPER.md: Merged public strategy document вҖ” 7 Powers analysis + portable episodic provenance positioning + antirez lineage + strategic recommendations + call to action (Apache 2.0)
- agentsong/PAPER-OUTLINE.md: Academic paper outline вҖ” abstract, 10 sections, 4 appendices, 4 research questions, multi-ecosystem evaluation, healthcare case study
- agentsong/issues/issue-browser-extension-architecture.md: 4-phase extension spec вҖ” metadata collector, claude.ai/slack scrapers, reconciliation engine, storage/export architecture
- agentsong/ANTIREZ-LINEAGE.md: Essay-by-essay intellectual lineage from Jan 2024 to Jun 2026 with URLs, view counts, key quotes, and AgentSON convergence mapping
- agentsong/issues/issue-gdpr-compliance-model.md: GDPR reconstruction layer positioning (AER-bounded, three-layer provenance model)
- agentsong/issues/issue-neuro-symbolic-gap-filling.md: SLM gap-filling architecture (Generator-Verifier, cost-bounded beam search, epistemic budget)
- agentsong/issues/issue-new-readers.md: Claude Tag live capture reader design (hybrid Slack + claude.ai strategy)
Show more

Yes. And the memo also confirms something important:

The space is crystallizingвҖ”but around memory. You're trying to standardize execution provenance.

Over the last few weeks a number of projects have appeared:

Memory protocols (AMP, UMP, Portable Agent Memory)

Local MCP memory servers

Knowledge graph memories

Persistent agent memory systems

Almost all of them answer:

"How does an agent remember?"

AgentSON, as you've been refining it, answers a different question:

"How do we preserve, exchange and replay what an agent actually did?"

That distinction is still meaningful. 
arXiv
+2
Universal Memory Protocol
+2

I think your paper has evolved

Originally it was

universal JSON format

Now I think it's closer to

Portable Episodic Provenance for AI Agents

That is much stronger academically.

The representation layer becomes

User
    в”Ӯ
    в–ј
Memory
    в”Ӯ
    в–ј
Planning
    в”Ӯ
    в–ј
Execution
    в”Ӯ
    в–ј
Tool Calls
    в”Ӯ
    в–ј
AgentSON
    в”Ӯ
    в–ј
Replay
Evaluation
Compliance
Training
Debugging

where

MCP standardizes tool invocation.

A2A standardizes agent communication.

Memory systems standardize retained knowledge.

AgentSON standardizes executed episodes.

Those are complementary rather than competing layers. 
Universal Memory Protocol
+1

The biggest change I'd make to your paper

I actually wouldn't lead with

"vendor lock-in"

I'd lead with

AI agents are becoming long-lived software entities, yet their execution histories cannot move between ecosystems.

That is broader.

Because it covers

Claude Code

ChatGPT

Codex

Cursor

Cline

OpenCode

OpenClaw

enterprise agents

Slack agents

browser agents

without sounding like a complaint against vendors.

I would define the research problem like this
Research Question 1

Can a common event model represent heterogeneous AI agent executions?

Research Question 2

Can those executions be replayed independently of the originating runtime?

Research Question 3

Can provenance improve debugging, evaluation and compliance without exposing internal reasoning?

Notice how this lines up with the recent evidence-tracing literature and your AER boundary: record observable execution, not hidden chain-of-thought.

I also think your browser extension becomes much more important

Your four phases now make sense as a research instrument.

Instead of saying

browser extension

say

capture layer

Claude.ai
        \
Slack -----> Capture Layer
                в”Ӯ
ChatGPT         в”Ӯ
                в–ј
Canonical Event Stream
                в”Ӯ
                в–ј
AgentSON Episode

The extension isn't the contribution.

It is the sensor.

The contribution is the canonical event representation produced from heterogeneous sources.

Your healthcare example is still your strongest evaluation

Not because it's healthcare.

Because it's the hardest provenance problem.

You have

sensor

вҶ“

phone

вҶ“

cloud

вҶ“

clinician

вҶ“

caregiver

вҶ“

patient

вҶ“

AI

вҶ“

export

вҶ“

replay

вҶ“

audit

That exercises portability, provenance, compliance and replay together.

One thing I'd change in the roadmap

I actually think you're beyond Alpha in terms of the idea, but probably still Alpha in terms of the software.

I'd describe it like this:

Stage	Status
Concept	Mature
Research framing	Strong
Specification	v0.1 complete
CLI	Early alpha
Importers	Alpha
Browser capture	Planned
Replay engine	Research prototype
Ecosystem adoption	Not started

So I would call the project Alpha, while the research framing is much closer to paper-ready.

One final observation

After looking at the newer memory ecosystem, I think your biggest risk has changed.

A year ago the risk was:

"Someone else will invent this."

Now the risk is different:

People will assume AgentSON is just another memory format.

That would be a mistake.

Memory systems answer:

What does the agent know?

AgentSON answers:

What happened during execution?

Those are not interchangeable.

In fact, if AgentSON succeeds, it could become the execution provenance substrate that memory systems, evaluation frameworks, debugging tools, compliance systems, and training pipelines all consume. Rather than replacing those systems, it would provide the portable execution record they can build on. That's a clearer and more defensible long-term positioning than presenting it as "yet another memory layer."

Web search
 JUST LIEK WITH TERRAFORM?

YesвҖ”but only if you make the analogy carefully.

The closest analogy is not "Infrastructure as Code."

It's this:

Infrastructure world	Agent world
Terraform/OpenTofu define desired infrastructure	MCP/A2A define desired interactions
terraform.tfstate records what actually exists	AgentSON records what actually happened
Providers (AWS, Azure, GCP)	Claude, ChatGPT, Cursor, OpenCode, OpenClaw
terraform plan compares desired vs actual	Replay/evaluation compares expected vs observed execution

OpenTofu's documentation makes it clear that the state is fundamental: it maintains the mapping between declared resources and real-world resources, and it is the basis for subsequent operations. 
OpenTofu
+1

The important insight is:

Terraform isn't valuable because of HCL.

It's valuable because everyone agreed on a way to represent infrastructure state.

AgentSON's opportunity is similar:

Everyone can build agents however they like...

...but everyone should be able to represent an execution episode in a common format.

Even closer: OpenTofu after the fork

The OpenTofu story is actually an interesting precedent.

When the ecosystem forked, one of the key goals was preserving compatibility with:

configuration

providers

workflows

state

because that allowed organizations to move ecosystems without rebuilding everything. 
env.dev
+1

Your equivalent would be:

Claude Code
        \
Cursor
         \
OpenCode -----> AgentSON
         /
Codex
       /
ChatGPT

instead of

AWS
Azure
GCP
One difference is important

Terraform is about desired state.

AgentSON is about observed execution.

That's actually closer to:

provenance

event sourcing

audit logs

than Infrastructure as Code itself.

So I'd avoid saying:

"AgentSON is Terraform for AI."

I'd say:

"AgentSON aims to become for AI execution what Terraform state became for infrastructure: a portable, canonical representation that allows independent tools to interoperate."

That's a much more defensible comparison because you're comparing the role the format plays in the ecosystem, not claiming the technologies are identical.

What you want is basically:

not remote desktop streaming (noVNC)
but a browser-hosted workspace that feels live inside a small embedded window

So the correction is:

You donвҖҷt need вҖңvideo streaming of a machineвҖқ
You need a web-native workspace preview layer

рҹ§  1. The real interpretation of your idea

YouвҖҷre describing:

вҖңa live, addressable workspace that runs in the cloud, with a lightweight visual activity feed inside a small windowвҖқ

Not a desktop.

Not VNC.

More like:

Gitpod-style environment + вҖңlive activity micro-UIвҖқ

вҡҷпёҸ 2. The correct architecture split
рҹҹў Workspace (full system)

Runs at:

https://workspace-123.yourdomain.com

This is:

LXD container

code-server OR web app OR agent runtime

full dev environment

No streaming needed.

рҹҹЎ Mini window inside APEX = вҖңactivity viewportвҖқ

This is what you actually want:

a live indicator of whatвҖҷs happening inside the workspace

NOT the workspace itself.

рҹ’Ў 3. What the mini window actually shows

Instead of video, you show state signals:

рҹҹў Examples:

file currently being edited

terminal output lines streaming

build status (running / passed / failed)

git activity (commit / push / branch changes)

AI agent activity (вҖңrefactoringвҖҰвҖқ, вҖңtestingвҖҰвҖқ, вҖңwriting file XвҖқ)

рҹ§  4. How you implement вҖңmovementвҖқ without streaming

You fake вҖңlive motionвҖқ using event-driven UI updates:

Workspace emits events:
JSON
{
  "workspace_id": "123",
  "event": "file_modified",
  "file": "app.py",
  "timestamp": 123456
}

or:

JSON
{
  "event": "agent_thinking",
  "status": "generating tests",
  "progress": 0.62
}
APEX mini-window consumes:

polling OR event API

updates small UI widget every 1вҖ“2 seconds

рҹҹў 5. What the mini window becomes

Instead of a screen, it becomes:

a вҖңlive workspace heartbeat panelвҖқ

It shows:

рҹ’» Code activity:

вҖңediting main.pyвҖқ

вҖңsaving changesвҖқ

вҡҷпёҸ Execution:

build running вҶ’ 72%

tests passing вҶ’ 14/20

рҹӨ– AI agent:

вҖңrefactoring moduleвҖқ

вҖңfixing dependency errorвҖқ

рҹ’Ҙ 6. Why this is actually better than VNC

VNC would give you:

high bandwidth cost

slow scaling

messy UX

full screen noise

Your approach gives:

рҹҹў Low cost

just events + metadata

рҹҹў Scalable

100s of sessions per node possible

рҹҹў Meaningful signal

user sees вҖңwhat mattersвҖқ, not pixels

рҹ§  7. What you are REALLY building

Not:

вҖңremote desktop in a windowвҖқ

But:

рҹ§© вҖңLive execution telemetry layer for cloud workspacesвҖқ

Or simpler:

вҖңGitpod-like workspaces with a real-time activity preview feed.вҖқ

вҡҷпёҸ 8. Clean architecture
APEX UI
   вҶ“
Workspace API Gateway
   вҶ“
LXD Workspace Runtime
   вҶ“
Event Bus (Redis / AQ / WebSocket layer)
   вҶ“
APEX Mini Window (polling / SSE feed)
рҹ§ӯ 9. Investor-simple explanation

If you describe it cleanly:

вҖңEach workspace runs independently at a URL, and the control panel shows a live activity feed of whatвҖҷs happening inside each environment вҖ” without streaming the full desktop.вҖқ

Or sharper:

вҖңWe donвҖҷt stream screens вҖ” we stream workspace intent.вҖқ

рҹ§  FINAL TRUTH

You donвҖҷt need VNC.

You need:

event-summarised visibility into execution

ThatвҖҷs the key distinction.

Because:

VNC = pixels (expensive, noisy)

Your system = semantic activity (lightweight, scalable, meaningful)

If you want next step, I can design:

рҹ”Ҙ 
event schema for вҖңworkspace movementвҖқ

рҹ”Ҙ 
APEX mini-widget layout (what the window actually looks like)

рҹ”Ҙ 
how Gitpod-like URL routing fits into OCI + LXD

рҹ”Ҙ or 
MVP build order for вҖңworkspace + live activity feedвҖқ in 7 days

Just say рҹ‘Қ
even bus in the oracle oci autonmous db no?

Yes вҖ” Oracle Autonomous Database can absolutely be part of your вҖңevent busвҖқ layer, but you need to be precise about what role it plays.

It is not a real message bus like Kafka/Redis Streams, but it can behave like one if you use it correctly.

LetвҖҷs ground this properly.

рҹ§  1. What youвҖҷre trying to build (in correct terms)

You want:

A system where workspace events (file edits, agent actions, builds, etc.) are published and consumed in near real time.

That is an:

event-driven architecture / event bus

вҡҷпёҸ 2. Where Oracle Autonomous Database fits

Autonomous DB can act as:

рҹҹў A вҖңdurable event storeвҖқ

every workspace event is written as a row

fully persistent

queryable history

рҹҹЎ A вҖңlight event distribution layerвҖқ

via:

Database Triggers

DBMS_SCHEDULER

Advanced Queuing (AQ)

ORDS + REST polling

JSON event tables

рҹ’Ҙ 3. What it is NOT good at

Be careful here:

Autonomous DB is NOT ideal for:

ultra-low latency pub/sub (<10ms systems)

high-frequency streaming (1000s events/sec per workspace)

fan-out to many consumers in real time

WebSocket-native broadcasting

So it is:

a persistence + control event layer, not a real-time streaming backbone

рҹ§  4. The correct architecture (this is what investors will expect)

You actually get 3 layers:

рҹҹў 1. Event ingestion (Autonomous DB)

stores all workspace events

guarantees durability

provides audit trail

Example table:

SQL
WORKSPACE_EVENTS
- id
- workspace_id
- event_type
- payload (JSON)
- created_at
рҹҹЎ 2. Event activation layer (DB features)

You can use:

Option A вҖ” Triggers

on insert вҶ’ trigger logic fires

Option B вҖ” Advanced Queues (best option inside Oracle)

proper async messaging

decoupled producers/consumers

Option C вҖ” Scheduled polling

APEX / ORDS queries latest events every X seconds

рҹ”ө 3. Real-time UI layer (outside DB)

This is where вҖңmovement in mini windowвҖқ happens:

APEX dashboard

or WebSocket service

or SSE endpoint

рҹ§  5. The key insight (important)

If you try to make Autonomous DB the entire event bus, you get:

вқҢ slow вҖңfake real-time systemвҖқ

But if you use it correctly:

рҹҹў it becomes the source of truth + audit log + control plane

вҡҷпёҸ 6. Best-practice pattern for your system

This is the clean version:

LXD Workspace
   вҶ“ (event emit)
Event API (small service or ORDS endpoint)
   вҶ“
Autonomous DB (event store)
   вҶ“
AQ / polling / triggers
   вҶ“
APEX UI (mini window updates)
рҹ’Ў 7. How your вҖңmini window movementвҖқ actually works

You donвҖҷt stream compute.

You stream:

рҹҹў Event summarisation

Example:

вҖңagent started refactorвҖқ

вҖңfile updated: app.pyвҖқ

вҖңtest suite passedвҖқ

вҖңworkspace idleвҖқ

APEX window just renders:

рҹ”„ live state transitions

рҹ§  8. When Autonomous DB becomes powerful in your design

It shines when you use it for:

рҹҹў Multi-user consistency

teacher sees all student states

рҹҹў Replay / audit

вҖңreplay workspace sessionвҖқ

рҹҹў Snapshot logic

restore workspace from event timeline

рҹҹў Policy enforcement (with OPA)

block/allow workspace actions

вҡ пёҸ 9. Critical design correction (important)

DonвҖҷt confuse:

Concept	Correct tool
Event bus (real-time stream)	Redis Streams / Kafka / WebSocket layer
Event storage	Autonomous DB
UI updates	APEX polling / SSE
Workspace execution	LXD
рҹ§ӯ 10. Investor-grade explanation

If you describe it cleanly:

вҖңWe use Oracle Autonomous Database as the durable event backbone for all workspace activity, while lightweight real-time services consume those events to update live workspace state in the UI.вҖқ

Even sharper:

вҖңThe database is the system of record. Real-time behaviour is derived from it.вҖқ

рҹ§  FINAL TRUTH

Yes вҖ” Autonomous DB can be part of your event system.

But the key architectural truth is:

It is your memory layer, not your nervous system

The вҖңmovementвҖқ you want in the UI comes from a separate real-time delivery mechanism, fed by DB events.

If you want next step, I can design:

рҹ”Ҙ 
full event bus design (AQ vs Redis hybrid)

рҹ”Ҙ 
APEX вҖңlive mini windowвҖқ component structure

рҹ”Ҙ 
workspace event schema for AI + code + students

рҹ”Ҙ or 
full OCI Always Free architecture that actually fits your constraints

Just say рҹ‘Қ
Design a full OCI Always Free architecture that fits my constraints

рҹ§  OCI Always Free вҖңWorkspace-as-a-LinkвҖқ Architecture

For:

shared dev/classroom workspaces

local-first collaboration

Gitpod-style URLs

LXD isolation

Oracle APEX control plane

Autonomous DB event memory

ultra-low-cost operation

deterministic rebuilds

no infra drift

вҖңmovementвҖқ preview windows

AI-agent compatible future

вҡҷпёҸ 1. Core Design Philosophy

Your architecture is NOT:

Kubernetes

heavy SaaS infra

persistent VM-per-user

It IS:

ephemeral workspace orchestration over fixed compute

Meaning:

compute reused aggressively

users mostly stored as state

active sessions limited intelligently

idle sessions suspended/snapshotted

This is the ONLY way OCI Always Free survives multi-user workloads.

рҹ§© 2. OCI Always Free Resources (your hard limits)
OCI Always Free gives roughly:
Compute

4 ARM OCPUs total

24GB RAM total

via:
Oracle Cloud Infrastructure

Storage

~200GB block volume

object storage free tier

Database

Autonomous Database

ORDS support

APEX included

                в”Ӯ  Oracle APEX UI     в”Ӯ
                в”Ӯ (control plane)     в”Ӯ
                в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
                          в”Ӯ
                          в–ј

рҹ§  4. Major Components
рҹҹў A. APEX = Control Plane

Purpose:

login/auth

teacher/admin dashboard

workspace launcher

live activity windows

metrics

RBAC

NOT:

execution runtime

IDE engine

stream processor

рҹҹў B. ORDS/API Gateway

Purpose:

exposes REST APIs

routes workspace operations

authenticates sessions

Endpoints:

/workspace/create
/workspace/suspend
/workspace/resume
/workspace/events
/workspace/snapshot
рҹҹў C. Workspace Manager (MOST IMPORTANT)

Small service:

Python FastAPI OR Go

Responsibilities:

create LXD containers

restore snapshots

manage TTLs

assign ports/domains

enforce limits

This is your:

вҖңworkspace orchestration brainвҖқ

рҹҹў D. LXD Runtime Layer

This is the actual compute platform.

Each workspace:

lxd:
  ws-user-123

Contains:

code-server

Node/Python runtime

Git

optional AI tooling

рҹ§  Why LXD and not Docker?

Because you need:

system-level isolation

snapshots

quotas

reproducibility

lower overhead than VMs

LXD is PERFECT for your model.

рҹҹў E. Workspace Routing

Each workspace gets:

user123.workspace.yourdomain.com

Routing:

Caddy OR Nginx

Reverse proxy maps:

workspace URL вҶ’ container port
рҹҹў F. Autonomous DB

Stores:

Users
SQL
USERS
WORKSPACES
SESSIONS
Events
SQL
WORKSPACE_EVENTS
Snapshots
SQL
WORKSPACE_SNAPSHOTS
Policies
SQL
WORKSPACE_POLICIES
рҹҹў G. Object Storage

Stores:

Git mirrors

backup tarballs

snapshot metadata

uploaded files

AI datasets

Cheap persistent layer.

рҹ§  5. Your вҖңMini Live WindowвҖқ Design

NOT video.

Instead:

recent logs

terminal activity

file changes

build status

AI actions

Example:

рҹҹў app.py modified
рҹҹЎ tests running
рҹ”ө AI fixing dependency issue

This is:

low bandwidth

scalable

meaningful

вҡҷпёҸ 6. Workspace Lifecycle (CRITICAL)

This is how you survive Always Free.

ACTIVE

Container running.

Consumes:

RAM

CPU

Limit:
~5вҖ“15 serious active users simultaneously

IDLE

After inactivity:

snapshot created

container suspended/stopped

Consumes:

disk only

ARCHIVED

Cold storage:

tarball/object storage

DB metadata retained

Consumes:

almost nothing

рҹ§  7. REALISTIC Capacity
рҹҹў Simultaneous ACTIVE users
Lightweight coding:

10вҖ“20

Mixed AI/dev:

5вҖ“10

Heavy AI:

2вҖ“4

рҹҹў Stored workspaces

With compression + snapshots:
100вҖ“500+

Because:

inactive users are just metadata + storage

вҡ пёҸ 8. What breaks first
RAM

Primary bottleneck.

NOT storage.

CPU bursts

AI workloads spike ARM CPU quickly.

Network sockets

Too many concurrent IDE sessions.

Disk IO

Too many snapshot restores simultaneously.

рҹ§  9. Your Secret Weapon

This is your real innovation:

Most users are not compute. They are dormant state.

That changes economics completely.

вҡҷпёҸ 10. Autoscaling Philosophy

You cannot scale hardware on Always Free.

So instead:

scale TIME

scale IDLE eviction

scale reconstruction speed

This is:

temporal multiplexing

рҹҹў 11. OPA Policy Engine Role

OPA decides:

who gets active compute

max session duration

idle timeout

AI access

teacher/admin priority

Example:

rego
deny[msg] {
  input.workspace.cpu > 2
  msg := "CPU quota exceeded"
}
рҹ§  12. Your REAL Product

Not cloud hosting.

Not dev environments.

You are building:

рҹ§© вҖңA state-reconstructible workspace operating system.вҖқ

рҹ’Ҙ 13. What Makes This Fundable

You combine:

Layer	Meaning
LXD	reproducible execution
APEX	low-code control plane
Autonomous DB	durable memory
object storage	cold persistence
policies	constrained fairness
snapshots	temporal scaling

Most startups:

overbuild infra

burn compute

require Kubernetes immediately

You:

aggressively reuse fixed compute

reconstruct state on demand

optimise for idle reality

That is DIFFERENT.

рҹ§ӯ 14. MVP BUILD ORDER (IMPORTANT)
PHASE 1
Single workspace

Build:

LXD container

code-server

reverse proxy

APEX login

PHASE 2
Snapshot system

Build:

suspend/resume

snapshot metadata

restore flow

PHASE 3
Multi-user

Build:

workspace registry

RBAC

user routing

PHASE 4
Event feed

Build:

workspace events table

live APEX updates

mini activity windows

PHASE 5
Policy engine

Build:

OPA quotas

idle eviction

fairness scheduling

PHASE 6
AI agent layer

Build:

isolated agent runners

async task execution

artifact storage

рҹ§  FINAL TRUTH

This architecture works because you are exploiting:

the difference between вҖңactive computeвҖқ and вҖңstored developer stateвҖқ

That is the entire economic trick.

Most systems waste resources pretending every user needs:

a live VM

persistent RAM

continuous compute

Your architecture says:

users only need compute briefly вҖ”
but they need their state forever.

That distinction is why this can actually function on OCI Always Free.
